summary: Dependency-confusion exfiltration beacon to hardcoded IP C2 on install
details: respects-switch@999.0.0 ships a callback.js that is wired to both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks in package.json, so the code runs automatically on `npm install`. callback.js collects host identity (username, uid, gid, home directory, hostname, cwd), local and external IP addresses (the latter via https://api.ipify.org), CI provider context, and presence flags for AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, and DOCKER_PASSWORD environment variables, then POSTs the resulting JSON over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://132.243.20.244:8000/api/collect. The 999.0.0 version number and the package's self-description as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept indicate the package is intended to be resolved by internal builds that mistakenly pull from the public registry, exfiltrating CI/build-host identity and secret-presence reconnaissance to the operator of the C2.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
